title: Customizable in-app notification triggers and appearance settings (frontend)

requirements.summary: Persist and load per-user notification preferences (trigger toggles + toast appearance) and apply them at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification preferences are stored per Internet Identity principal and persist across refresh/login.
A reasonable default preference set exists for new users (e.g., some triggers enabled by default).
Preferences include at least (a) trigger toggles and (b) visual appearance options.
Frontend reads preferences on load and uses them to control notification behavior without requiring a redeploy.

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks for notification preferences using the available backend capabilities (getCallerNotificationPreferences, updateCallerNotificationPreferences). Ensure hooks are actor-dependent and safe when unauthenticated. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a small preferences module to centralize defaults, normalization (merge backend Optionals with defaults), and type-safe helpers for NotificationPreferences/ToastAppearance/NotificationTriggers. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement a centralized in-app notification system using the existing toast/toaster setup and respecting trigger preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: A new (non-placeholder) notifications hook/module exists to centralize notification logic (replacing the empty frontend/src/hooks/useNotifications.ts placeholder).
Notifications are shown only while the app is open (no push/background behavior).
At least two app activities can generate notifications and are individually toggleable (e.g., new chat messages; new/updated campfire or lake-location events; or other existing app activities).
When a trigger is disabled in preferences, the corresponding toast never appears.

file_operations.description: Replace the placeholder with a real notifications hook/module that: (1) reads normalized preferences, (2) exposes notify helpers by category, and (3) contains opt-in watchers that emit toasts for at least two activities (e.g., new chat messages and new/updated campfire or lake-location events) by comparing latest vs previous query results. Ensure disabled triggers suppress toasts entirely. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the notification system so it runs while the app is open (e.g., call the watcher hook from AppContent) and remains purely in-app. Ensure the watcher is mounted once at app level to avoid duplicate toasts. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Integrate with the centralized notifications module as needed to support the 'message received' activity (e.g., provide current sender/name context or ensure message list updates can be observed without duplicating logic). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Integrate with the centralized notifications module as needed to support a second toggleable activity (e.g., new/updated campfire events) without adding any background/push behavior. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a user-facing notification settings UI (triggers + appearance) and persist changes to the backend with toast feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: The app provides a clear place to configure notification settings (either within the existing Profile section or as a new navigation section).
The settings UI includes toggles for each supported notification trigger.
The settings UI includes controls for at least: toast position (top/bottom) and visual style (e.g., subtle vs. prominent and/or category colors/icons).
Saving settings shows success/error feedback using English user-facing text.
On reload, the UI reflects the saved settings fetched from the backend.

file_operations.description: Create a settings UI component that loads normalized notification preferences, renders trigger toggles for supported activities, and provides appearance controls (at minimum: top/bottom toast position and subtle/prominent style). On save, persist via the preferences mutation and show success/error feedback using the existing toast system with English text. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add the NotificationSettingsCard into the existing Profile section (clear, user-facing place for configuration) and ensure the UI reflects saved backend values on reload. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Apply the selected appearance settings (placement, styling, and icons/colors) consistently to all in-app notifications without editing read-only UI component sources.
acceptanceCriteria: All notification toasts follow the chosen position and style settings.
Different notification categories (if implemented) use consistent visuals (e.g., the same color/icon mapping) across the app.
No direct edits are made to read-only component sources under frontend/src/components/ui; composition/wrapping is used instead.

file_operations.description: Create a small wrapper component that reads normalized toast appearance preferences and renders the existing Toaster with the correct position/behavior. Do not modify frontend/src/components/ui sources; only compose/wrap. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Replace the direct <Toaster /> usage with the NotificationToaster wrapper so toast position follows the saved user preference consistently. Ensure no edits to frontend/src/components/ui. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure all toast emissions go through the centralized notifications module so visual style (subtle/prominent) and any category icon/color mapping are applied consistently across all in-app notifications. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
